title: Saffron-Infused Potato Gratin with Gruyère & Thyme
summary: A bubbling, golden brown potato gratin with a delicate saffron twist. This one is an absolute crowd pleaser.

method.description: Heat the oven to 190°C / 375°F. Grease a baking dish with butter. Thinly slice the potatoes and onions. Mince the garlic. Grate the Gruyère cheese.
To infuse the Saffron, warm the milk in a small saucepan and add the saffron threads. Let it steep for about 10 mins.

Meanwhile, in the prepared dish, layer half of the potatoes, onions, and garlic. Season with salt, pepper, and thyme. Sprinkle with half of the Gruyère cheese. Repeat with remaining potatoes, onions, garlic, and cheese.
Combine the cream with the saffron-infused milk, and pour it evenly over the potatoes.
Dot the top with butter and bake for 45-60 mins, until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden brown.
Let the gratin cool slightly before serving.

chefs.name: Laurent Dagenais
chefs.summary:
Born and raised in Montreal, Dagenais is a chef by training who worked in the restaurant industry for almost a decade before a snowboarding accident changed his trajectory. “A decade ago my career took a turn that I would have never predicted,” explained Dagenais. “Breaking my arm put my cooking life on hold and that's why, in the midst of the pandemic, I started making videos on the internet.”
 
It was these videos that changed his life, making him an instant social media phenomenon. Reaching over 4+ million followers across his channels, Dagenais' main goal is to share his love of food and inspire people to get in to the kitchen more often.
